title: Psychiatry referral documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports clinician-documented supportive discussion, questions, safety planning, treatment or medication decisions, monitoring, follow-up, and referral without autonomous treatment selection or doses.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Depression
source.population: Adults presenting with suspected or established depressive symptoms; emergency, severe, psychotic, bipolar, or safeguarding presentations require escalation.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth depression documentation and risk screening; treatment, diagnosis, and local emergency pathways remain clinician-led.

assessment.text: Supports documenting severe functional impairment, self-harm or suicide risk, substance misuse, self-neglect, treatment history, home and community support, family impact, referral, and collaborative care planning.
assessment.rationale: Supports specialist assessment for high-risk GAD presentations for psychiatry referral documentation; no decision is generated.

source.title: Generalised anxiety disorder and panic disorder in adults: management — Recommendations
source.population: Adults with known or suspected generalised anxiety disorder or panic disorder.
source.setting: Primary, community, and specialist mental-health care.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult GAD identification, assessment, stepped care, risk, and clinician-led management; not exact for children, every anxiety subtype, or UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.37–1.2.39 — high risk and specialist assessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting severe functional impairment, self-harm or suicide risk, substance misuse, self-neglect, treatment history, home and community support, family impact, referral, and collaborative care planning.

assessment.text: Supports recording the person’s needs, concerns, strengths, vulnerabilities, context and clinician-performed psychosocial assessment without using a prediction score.
assessment.rationale: Supports psychosocial assessment and care context after self-harm for psychiatry referral documentation; no decision is generated.

source.title: Self-harm: assessment, management and preventing recurrence
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; requires UAE legal and pathway adaptation
source.population: Children, young people and adults who have self-harmed, unless a recommendation is age-specific.
source.setting: Community, emergency department, hospital and mental-health care following self-harm.
source.exclusions: Exact for compassionate psychosocial assessment, emergency-department initial assessment, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness for physical and mental care, care-plan status, observation and discharge documentation after self-harm. It does not apply a risk score, predict suicide or generate treatment.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording the person’s needs, concerns, strengths, vulnerabilities, context and clinician-performed psychosocial assessment without using a prediction score.
